html:
Exclusive to:

The Flytrap Stalk is a Food Item exclusive to the Hamlet DLC. It is dropped only by fully grown Snaptooth Flytraps and when consumed, restores 12.5 Hunger and depletes the player's Sanity by 10. Flytrap Stalks cannot be cooked or used in the Crock Pot, but can be dried on a Drying Rack to turn them into Stalking Sticks (the Hamlet equivalent of the Walking Cane), or used to craft the Bug B'Gone repellent and the Root Trunk.

It is the only Meat Food Item that can be set on fire.

Usage

×3

×6

×2

×3

Trivia

  • The Flytrap Stalk used not to be flammable. This was later changed in the February 13, 2019 update.
By TypeBy Crock Pot Value

The Flytrap Stalk is a Food Item exclusive to the Hamlet DLC. It is dropped only by fully grown Snaptooth Flytraps and when consumed, restores 12.5 Hunger and depletes the player's Sanity by 10. Flytrap Stalks cannot be cooked or used in the Crock Pot, but can be dried on a Drying Rack to turn them into Stalking Sticks (the Hamlet equivalent of the Walking Cane), or used to craft the Bug B'Gone repellent and the Root Trunk.
It is the only Meat Food Item that can be set on fire.

The '''Flytrap Stalk''' is a [[Food]] [[Item]] exclusive to the ''[[Don't Starve: Hamlet|Hamlet]]'' DLC. It is dropped only by fully grown [[Snaptooth|Snaptooth Flytraps]] and when consumed, restores 12.5 [[Hunger]] and depletes the player's [[Sanity]] by 10. Flytrap Stalks cannot be cooked or used in the [[Crock Pot]], but can be dried on a [[Drying Rack]] to turn them into [[Stalking Stick]]s (the ''Hamlet'' equivalent of the [[Walking Cane]]), or used to craft the [[Bug B'Gone]] repellent and the [[Root Trunk]].

It is the only [[Meat]] [[Food]] [[Item]] that can be set on fire.

* The Flytrap Stalk used not to be flammable. This was later changed in the February 13, 2019 update.
